description: Hand-labeled retrieval eval pairs for fbrain. Each pair declares a (query, expected_slug, expected_type) triple plus a `seed` block the harness uses to materialise the record on demand. The slug prefix `eval-retrieval-` is reserved for this harness so seeding/teardown can't collide with real records. See README 'Quality / eval' and docs/phase-7-search-latency-spike.md G3b.

pairs.query: distributed cache invalidation across regions

seed.title: Octopus — multi-region cache invalidation
seed.body: Design for invalidating a distributed cache across geographic regions when a write lands in one shard. Walks the fan-out pattern, the back-channel, and the failure mode where a region misses the broadcast.

pairs.notes: Body keyword 'fan-out' overlaps several records; the design-specific phrase is 'multi-region cache'.

pairs.query: implement cache eviction policy

seed.title: Blueberry — implement cache eviction
seed.body: Pick an eviction policy (LRU vs LFU vs ARC) and wire it into the in-memory cache layer. Includes benchmarks for hit rate under skewed workloads.

pairs.notes: Pairs with octopus on 'cache' — eval-mode dedup means rank-1 should be the task because the query mentions eviction.

pairs.query: backpressure when producers outpace consumers

seed.title: Canyon — backpressure handling
seed.body: What to do when an upstream producer emits faster than the downstream consumer can drain. Covers buffering, dropping, and pushing the slowdown signal back through the pipe.

pairs.query: never silently swallow errors

seed.title: Elder — surface every error
seed.body: When in doubt, log loud. A silent catch is a future incident in a costume. This preference says: even handled errors get a structured log entry; raw try/catch with bare 'return null' is not OK.

pairs.query: Postgres logical replication documentation

seed.title: Fjord — Postgres replication docs
seed.body: Pointer to the canonical Postgres logical replication guide. Covers publications, subscriptions, replication slots, and the typical 'subscription lag growing unbounded' debugging path.

pairs.query: kanban orchestrator agent that manages task lifecycle

seed.title: Gondola — kanban orchestrator
seed.body: Agent that picks up tasks from the kanban board, runs them in isolated worktrees, and shepherds the resulting PR through merge. Owns the lifecycle handbook and the minion-checkpoint protocol.

pairs.query: Q3 search latency improvement project

seed.title: Harbor — Q3 search latency
seed.body: Project to cut p99 search latency from 800 ms to under 200 ms by Q3. Workstreams: native-index pruning, embedding cache, query rewriting, hybrid retrieval baseline.

pairs.query: spike investigating why search returns junk after deletes

seed.title: Iceberg — search-latency spike root cause
seed.body: Spike to investigate why semantic search produces irrelevant top results after a series of soft-deletes. Hypothesis: tombstoned-record embeddings stay in the native index and drown live records.

pairs.query: TLS termination at the edge load balancer

seed.title: Jaguar — TLS at the edge
seed.body: Terminate TLS at the edge proxy rather than the application server. Reduces fan-out of certificates and centralises rotation. Trade-off: edge needs access to private keys.

pairs.query: rotate signing keys on a quarterly schedule

seed.title: Kestrel — rotate signing keys quarterly
seed.body: Set up the quarterly key-rotation calendar event, automate the rotation through the KMS workflow, and verify all downstream services pick up the new key within 24 hours.

pairs.query: eventual consistency tradeoffs in distributed systems

seed.title: Lichen — eventual consistency tradeoffs
seed.body: Why eventual consistency wins over strong consistency in most user-facing systems: better availability, partition tolerance, and tail latency. Costs: read-your-writes anomalies and merge logic on the client.

pairs.query: prefer explicit code over implicit magic

seed.title: Mango — explicit over implicit
seed.body: Default to explicit imports, explicit types, explicit error paths. Magic globals and decorators that mutate behavior at distance are forbidden unless they save more reading than they cost.

pairs.query: Kubernetes networking and service mesh primer

seed.title: Nimbus — Kubernetes networking guide
seed.body: Reference doc for Kubernetes networking: pod CIDRs, services, Ingress, the CNI plugin model, and a survey of common service-mesh implementations (Linkerd, Istio).

pairs.query: automated code review agent that suggests improvements

seed.title: Orchid — code review automation
seed.body: Agent that watches incoming pull requests, runs a battery of static checks, and posts inline suggestions. Adversarial mode tries to break the diff before approving.

pairs.query: rewrite onboarding documentation for new hires

seed.title: Papaya — onboarding documentation overhaul
seed.body: Project to rewrite the new-hire onboarding doc set. Current docs are stale, scattered, and have a 30% bounce rate. Goals: single landing page, working examples, week-1 checklist.

pairs.query: websocket reconnection with exponential backoff

seed.title: Quasar — websocket reconnection
seed.body: Spike on resilient websocket reconnection. Survey of jitter strategies, exponential backoff caps, and how to differentiate transient network blips from permanent server-side disconnects.

pairs.query: rate limiting middleware design with token bucket

seed.title: Ravine — rate limiting middleware
seed.body: Design for a per-API-key rate limiting middleware using the token bucket algorithm. Bucket state lives in Redis; refill happens lazily on read.

pairs.query: migrate Stripe webhook handlers to new API version

seed.title: Saxon — migrate Stripe webhooks
seed.body: Migrate the Stripe webhook handlers from the 2022-11-15 API version to 2024-06-20. Backward-compatibility shims for event payload changes, plus replay testing against captured fixtures.

pairs.query: back of envelope capacity planning math

seed.title: Tundra — back-of-envelope capacity
seed.body: Heuristics for sizing a service before you have data. Requests-per-second times bytes-per-request gives bandwidth; cache hit rate times memory-per-entry gives footprint. Be off by 2x and ship.

pairs.query: pin dependencies in the lockfile, no floating versions

seed.title: Umbra — pin dependencies in lockfile
seed.body: Floating dependency versions in production are a footgun. Every dep gets a pinned version in the lockfile; renovate bumps them on a weekly cadence with CI gating.

pairs.query: OpenTelemetry attribute naming conventions

seed.title: Violet — OpenTelemetry attribute conventions
seed.body: Pointer to the OpenTelemetry semantic conventions doc. Standardised keys for HTTP, RPC, database, and messaging spans. Use these instead of inventing attribute names per service.

pairs.query: incident response runbook executor agent

seed.title: Walrus — incident response executor
seed.body: Agent that reads a runbook page on PagerDuty alert, executes the suggested mitigations in a sandboxed shell, and reports outcomes back to the incident channel. Human-in-the-loop on destructive steps.
